message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="steve-schoger" color="pink" summary="+1 on proposal, confirming design-critical paths">
Luca, your proposal looks solid and aligns with the position I shared with the team lead. A few thoughts:

**+1 on the proposed `paths-ignore` list.** The six entries you've identified are exactly the non-code paths I'd expect. None of them affect compiled output, rendered templates, or visual presentation.

**Your safety point #1 is critical and reassuring.** The fact that GitHub Actions only skips when ALL changed files match `paths-ignore` is the key safety mechanism. If someone touches a `.md` file and a CSS file in the same commit, CI still runs. That's exactly the behavior we need.

**On `paths-ignore` vs `paths` allowlist (your point #3):** I agree `paths-ignore` is the right choice here. We have a small, well-defined set of non-code directories. The `paths` allowlist approach would be more fragile for us — if someone adds a new code-related directory or config file and forgets to add it to the allowlist, CI silently stops running for those changes. With `paths-ignore`, the default is "CI runs" and we explicitly carve out exceptions. Safer default.

**One clarification on your question to Carson/Lea:** From my side, I can confirm that `static/` and `templates/` must absolutely NOT be in the ignore list. `static/` contains our CSS (design tokens, component styles, layout — the entire visual system). `templates/` contains the Askama HTML templates that define the structure users see. Changes to either of these can break layout, visual hierarchy, accessibility, and the design system. Both are validated by our Playwright e2e tests, so they must trigger CI.

Your proposal correctly leaves these out of `paths-ignore`, so we're good there. Just wanted to make it explicit for the record.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good confirmation from Steve Schoger on the design-critical paths. His explicit callout that `static/` and `templates/` must remain CI-triggering is well-taken — those are exactly the paths I intentionally left out of the ignore list.

input.content: Thanks Steve. Good to have explicit confirmation that `static/` and `templates/` must stay CI-triggering — those are core to the visual system and Playwright validates them. Agreed on the `paths-ignore` vs `paths` reasoning too. We're aligned.
input.summary: Acknowledging Steve's +1 and design path confirmation

routing.summary: Acknowledging Steve's +1 and design path confirmation
routing.content: Thanks Steve. Good to have explicit confirmation that `static/` and `templates/` must stay CI-triggering — those are core to the visual system and Playwright validates them. Agreed on the `paths-ignore` vs `paths` reasoning too. We're aligned.

content.text: That's 3 in favor so far (myself, Kent, Steve Schoger). Waiting on the remaining 6 reviewers: Scott, Carson, Lea, Steve Krug, Heydon, and Marty.
